# OverlapNet: a siamese network for computing LiDAR scan similarity with applications to loop closing and localization

- **Work ID:** `LPR-063`
- **Reviewed version:** `doi:10.1007/s10514-021-09999-0`

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** KITTI Odometry, Ford Campus, IPB-Car/Bonn seasonal dataset — `source_fact`; manual, p. 10–13, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
- **input_unit:** pair of single spherical LiDAR range images, global localization compares a query scan with map-rendered virtual scans — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–10, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
- **invariances:** none, relative yaw is explicitly estimated rather than eliminated — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 5–9, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
- **representation:** spherical range-image channels for range, normals, intensity and optional semantic probabilities, weight-sharing Siamese legs, overlap regression head, correlation-based relative-yaw head, SuMa loop-candidate integration, OverlapNet observation model for Monte Carlo localization — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–10, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–18, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–18, § Abstract;III. Method;IV-VI. Experiments, Fig. 2-19;Table I-IV
